Metabolic_traits: 970 lipid species and 267 fatty acid composite measures
Platform: MS (Metabolon Complex Lipids platform; BUME extraction + LC-MS)
Study_population: Rhineland Study (Germany); validation in FinnGen and EPIC-Potsdam
snippet: we perform genome-wide association studies on 970 lipid species and 267 fatty acid composite measures
notes: Preprint twin at 10.1101/2024.12.04.24318368 is not in existing_refs. Platform confirmed from methods: Metabolon Complex Lipids platform with BUME extraction and LC-MS. Population is European (German cohort + Finnish).

Study_population: Han Chinese individuals; cross-ancestry meta-analysis with European population
snippet: We performed large-scale genome-wide association studies of 171 directly quantifiable metabolites from a nuclear magnetic resonance-based metabolomics platform in 10,792 Han Chinese individuals
notes: Biofluid not stated in abstract (likely plasma/serum given NMR metabolomics context, but cannot confirm). N_loci not provided in truncated abstract. 15 variant-metabolite associations at least are mentioned from the discovery cohort.

Study_population: 254,825 participants (population not further specified in abstract)
snippet: We conduct a genome-wide association study (GWAS) identifying 24,438 independent variant-metabolite associations across 427 loci, with effect sizes highly concordant with 19 previous studies
notes: N_traits = 249 measures + 64 ratios = 313 total. Biofluid inferred as plasma from title ('plasma metabolome'). Ancestry not specified in truncated abstract.; possible overlap with existing UKB-NMR entries (Zoodsma / Tambets) — verify distinct

snippet: We conducted the largest genome-wide association study in East Asians to date, quantifying 248 blood metabolic biomarkers using nuclear magnetic resonance spectroscopy in 37,969 Japanese individuals
notes: DOI prefix 10.64898 is unusual (possibly new publisher/preprint server); DOI resolved 200. No PMID. N_loci not in truncated abstract. Journal listed as PPR (preprint). 8 novel associations mentioned.

Study_population: Hispanic Community Health Study/Study of Latinos (HCHS/SOL), 58.6% female, mean age 46.1 years
snippet: We performed a genome-wide association study (GWAS) of 81 fatty acids and oxylipins in 11,584 Hispanic Community Health Study/Study of Latinos (HCHS/SOL) participants
notes: Biofluid stated as 'circulating levels' - likely plasma/serum; not explicitly specified in abstract. Platform inferred as MS (lipidomics) - not named in truncated abstract. N_loci not in truncated abstract.

Metabolic_traits: 19 circulating fatty acid traits (polyunsaturated, monounsaturated, saturated)
Study_population: UK Biobank European ancestry (primary) plus five other ancestries
snippet: we conducted GWAS to identify additional genetic loci for 19 circulating FA traits in UK Biobank participants
notes: Published version of preprint 10.1101/2024.11.11.24317110. Platform confirmed as Nightingale NMR via UK Biobank metabolomics protocol. N_loci counts are per FA class; abstract gives 70+61+54 novel loci within those totals.

Reference: Wang et al., Am J Hum Genet 2025
Study_population: NHLBI TOPMed Program; ancestrally diverse (multi-ethnic) cohorts
snippet: performed a genome-wide association analysis on 1,135 selected metabolites using whole-genome sequencing data from 16,359 individuals
notes: Multi-ancestry study leveraging TOPMed WGS data. Platform is Metabolon (co-author affiliation: Metabolon Inc., and confirmed by paper context). Includes 85 sex-dimorphic locus-metabolite pairs. Harmonized catalog covers 1,729 metabolites across 25,058 samples.

Reference: van der Meer et al., medRxiv 2024
Metabolic_traits: 249 circulating plasma metabolic markers (Nightingale NMR panel)
Study_population: White British UK Biobank participants; replication in UK Biobank
snippet: We performed univariate and multivariate genome-wide association studies (GWAS) on the Nightingale panel of 249 circulating plasma metabolic markers, across 207,836 White British UK Biobank participants
notes: Preprint (medRxiv). N_loci not provided in truncated abstract. Focuses on pleiotropy and sex-specific genetic architecture. No published twin in existing_refs.txt.; possible overlap with existing UKB-NMR entries (Karjalainen 2024 / Tambets / Zoodsma) — verify distinct

Reference: Cai et al., J Biomed Res 2026
snippet: Genome-wide association study (GWAS) analyses of 846 plasma metabolites were performed in 495 Han Chinese individuals to identify mQTLs.
notes: DOI returns HTTP 200 (journal of biomedical research). Platform not specified in abstract. Very small N (495) — discovery only with no replication cohort mentioned in abstract. N_loci not given in truncated abstract. Qualifies as primary mQTL analysis of human plasma metabolite panel (846 metabolites). DOI resolves, PMID 42135028 also resolves on PubMed.

Metabolic_traits: 14 unique urinary metabolites (alanine, 3-aminoisobutyrate, betaine, creatine, creatinine, formate, glycine, glycolate, histidine, 2-hydroxybutyrate, lysine, threonine, trimethylamine, tyrosine)
Platform: Various (meta-analysis of existing GWAS; primarily NMR-based studies)
Study_population: Meta-analysis of multiple cohorts (max N=5,319 for glycine)
N_Loci: 48 independent lead SNPs across 14 metabolites; 4 novel (1 novel locus for tyrosine, 3 newly associated SNPs in known loci)
snippet: first comprehensive meta-analysis of urinary metabolite GWAS studies, aiming to consolidate existing data on metabolite-SNP associations
notes: Published version of preprint 10.1101/2024.06.25.600593 (not in existing_refs). This is a GWAS meta-analysis, not a primary new GWAS; N_traits and N_loci are based on abstract. Platform is heterogeneous across contributing studies. Qualifies as mQTL analysis aggregating urinary metabolomics panel GWAS data.

Study_population: 229 older adults with clinical AD and 322 age-matched healthy controls (USA)
journal: Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association
snippet: Unbiased associations between 6881 metabolites and 332,772 common genetic variants were tested, adjusted for age, sex, and both metabolomic and genomic principal components.
notes: DOI returns HTTP 403 but PMID 41236724 resolves on PubMed (HTTP 200). Platform not specified in abstract — untargeted MS inferred from panel size (6881 metabolites) but not confirmed. N_loci not extractable from truncated abstract. Disease-stratified cohort (AD patients + controls), not a general-population study; mQTL analysis using WGS and 332,772 common variants is genome-wide. Qualifies as primary mQTL study of human metabolite panel.

Study_population: Han Chinese individuals (discovery + independent Chinese cohort)
snippet: We quantified 2,759 serum metabolites and performed genome-wide association studies (GWASs) among Han Chinese individuals. We identified 184 study-wide significant metabolite quantitative trait loci.
notes: Platform not specified in abstract — large untargeted panel (2759 metabolites) suggests LC-MS or similar untargeted MS, but cannot confirm without full text. Cohort N not given in truncated abstract. 184 metaboQTLs reported; 88.59% (163) novel. DOI resolves (HTTP 200).

Study_population: Northern Finland Birth Cohort 1966 (NFBC 1966) and NFBC 1986 (Finnish)
snippet: We have analysed and reported CNVs in over 9,300 individuals and characterised their dosage effect (CNV-metabolomic QTL) on 228 plasma lipoproteins and metabolites.
notes: This is a CNV-metabolomic QTL study, not a traditional SNP-based GWAS. Platform inferred as NMR/Nightingale based on Finnish cohort standard practice and the description of lipoproteins+metabolites (228 measures), but not explicitly stated in abstract. N_loci not given in truncated abstract. Study is borderline: it is genome-wide in the CNV sense, and phenotypes are a metabolite panel in humans. Second part of paper reports metabolomic signatures for COVID-19 GWAS loci; the primary mQTL analysis on 228 metabolites qualifies.

snippet: "exome and genome-wide association study (Ex/GWAS) of 1,015 metabolites in serum samples from 5,981 Amish adults"
notes: rescued: agent rejected only for DOI 403 (bot-block); preprint; platform not stated in abstract

Metabolic_traits: untargeted and widely targeted serum metabolites and ratios
Platform: MS (untargeted and widely targeted; specific platform not named in abstract)
snippet: Genome-wide association studies (GWAS) were performed on all metabolites identified.
notes: DOI returns 403 (Cloudflare challenge / paywall); PMID 41938174 resolves on PubMed. Primary aim is identifying blood pressure-associated metabolites; GWAS of metabolite panel is a component, not the sole focus. MR performed downstream. N=1188 is small for GWAS. Platform not named (untargeted + widely targeted MS implied). N_traits and N_loci not available from truncated abstract — marked '?'. Borderline inclusion: qualifies as a primary GWAS of a metabolite panel in humans, but underpowered and not metabolomics-GWAS-first in framing.
